# 🚨 MANDATORY: Team Memory System 🚨

A shared team memory stores critical gotchas, conventions, and debugging insights across sessions.

## REQUIRED: /recall BEFORE Starting Any Task

You MUST run /recall at the start of every task to check for relevant prior knowledge. Skipping this risks repeating known mistakes and wasting time on already-solved problems.

```
/recall "<topic or keyword relevant to your task>"
```

Examples:
- Working on Stripe integration? → /recall "Stripe"
- Fixing a database query? → /recall "database" or /recall "Drizzle"
- Debugging a test? → /recall "testing patterns"
- Working on team creation? → /recall "team" or /recall "createId"
- Touching waivers? → /recall "waiver"

**Do NOT skip this step.** Past sessions have stored critical gotchas that will save significant debugging time.

## REQUIRED: /remember After Discoveries

When you discover something useful — a gotcha, root cause, convention, or debugging insight — you MUST save it immediately with /remember. Do not wait until the end of the session.

```
/remember "<what you learned>" --category=gotcha|convention|debugging|architecture|workflow --priority=critical|moderate|ephemeral
```

## Summary

- **BEFORE work**: /recall to load relevant memories (MANDATORY)
- **DURING work**: /remember as soon as you discover something worth saving (MANDATORY)
- Failure to use team memory means the team loses knowledge and repeats mistakes.

## deepwiki
DeepWiki MCP provides AI-powered documentation for GitHub repositories.

Available tools:
- read_wiki_structure: Get a list of documentation topics for a repository
- read_wiki_contents: View full documentation about a repository
- ask_question: Ask any question about a repository and get an AI-powered answer
- list_available_repos: List your available repositories (private mode only)
- generate_wiki: Generate a codebase wiki for a repository — only use when explicitly requested by the user (private mode only)
- devin_knowledge_manage: Manage Devin knowledge notes and suggestions — list, search, get, create, update, delete notes, view folder structure, list/view/dismiss knowledge suggestions (private mode only)
- devin_playbook_manage: Manage Devin playbooks — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_automation_scratch_manage: Read and update the shared scratch document attached to an automation — read, str_replace (also initialises an empty scratch), read_version (private mode only)
- devin_schedule_manage: Manage scheduled Devin sessions — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_session_create: Create one or more child Devin sessions (private mode only)
- devin_session_interact: Manage a Devin session — get status, send messages, sleep/terminate/archive, read messages & attachments, manage tags (private mode only)
- devin_session_events: Inspect session events — list summaries, fetch full details, or search event contents (private mode only)
- devin_session_search: Search and filter Devin sessions (private mode only)
- list_integrations: List all native integrations and MCP servers with their status and settings URLs (private mode only)

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.

Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.

**Add `shouldUnregister: true` to prevent stale values from breaking validation.**

When switching between kind options, RHF retains values for conditionally hidden fields (e.g., `sourceCompetitionId`, `sourceGroupId`, `globalSpots`). Without `shouldUnregister`, these stale values persist in form state and cause the "exactly one source" validation to fail, blocking submit.

<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
   const form = useForm<InviteSourceFormValues, unknown, InviteSourceFormValues>({
     resolver: standardSchemaResolver(inviteSourceFormSchema),
+    shouldUnregister: true,
     defaultValues: {
       kind:
         (defaultValues?.kind as InviteSourceFormValues["kind"]) ?? "competition",
```
</details>

Also, convert `InviteSourceFormValues` from a type to an interface per TS/TSX guidelines:

```diff
-export type InviteSourceFormValues = {
+export interface InviteSourceFormValues {
   kind: "competition" | "series" | "series_global"
   sourceCompetitionId?: string
   sourceGroupId?: string
   globalSpots?: number
   notes?: string
-}
+}
```

> ## Source details page
>
> ADR-0012 Phase 3: per-source details route at [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/invites/sources/$sourceId.tsx]] — hosts source meta editing plus the per-(championship division) allocation override table. The Sources tab's Edit button navigates here; the Add flow keeps the existing modal.
>
> Two write surfaces. Source meta (kind, source comp/series, default spots, notes) calls [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-invite-fns.ts#updateInviteSourceFn]] via the shared `InviteSourceForm` used by the Add modal. The per-division allocation table renders one row per championship division with a "Use default" checkbox and a numeric override input; saving calls [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-invite-fns.ts#saveInviteSourceAllocationsFn]] with `null` for "use default" rows (deletes the override) and a number ≥ 0 for explicit overrides (upserts the row).
>
> Loader gates on `MANAGE_COMPETITIONS` via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-invite-fns.ts#getInviteSourceByIdFn]] — a new single-source read that resolves the championship organizing team from the source row — and runs `Promise.all` for the source row, championship divisions, the championship-wide allocation map, and the source pickers. Discard restores the loader's seed values; Save invalidates the route on success.
>

1	/**
2	 * Championship Roster Computation
3	 *
4	 * `getChampionshipRoster` returns the union of every (sourceComp ×
5	 * sourceDivision) leaderboard for the championship's configured sources,
6	 * with each row tagged by the originating competition + division and the
7	 * athlete's email hydrated. The roster page renders this as a single
8	 * table with Competition + Division columns; client-side filters narrow
9	 * the view without round-tripping.
10	 *
11	 * Fan-out: a championship with K sources, each resolving to N
12	 * competitions × M divisions per competition, costs roughly K·N·M
13	 * leaderboard reads, fired in parallel. MVP organizers have 1–3 sources
14	 * with similarly small N/M, so the worst case is bounded under typical
15	 * usage. Leaderboards are cached on the public-facing render path.
16	 *
17	 * ADR-0012 Phase 6: per-(source, championshipDivision) cutoffs are
18	 * applied after leaderboards are fetched. The resolved allocation map
19	 * (`resolveSourceAllocations`) supplies the truncation N for each
20	 * `(sourceId, championshipDivisionId)`. Source-side `globalSpots` is
21	 * the per-division default when no override row exists. See ADR-0012
22	 * "Allocation Resolution".
23	 */
24	// @lat: [[competition-invites#Roster computation]]
25	
26	import { inArray } from "drizzle-orm"
27	import { getDb } from "@/db"
28	import {
29	  COMPETITION_INVITE_SOURCE_KIND,
30	  type CompetitionInviteSource,
31	  type CompetitionInviteSourceKind,
32	} from "@/db/schemas/competition-invites"
33	import { competitionsTable } from "@/db/schemas/competitions"
34	import { scalingLevelsTable } from "@/db/schemas/scaling"
35	import { parseCompetitionSettings } from "@/server-fns/competition-divisions-fns"
36	import type { ResolvedSourceAllocations } from "./allocations"
37	import { getCandidatesForSourceComp } from "./candidates"
38	import { type DivisionMapping, listSourcesForChampionship } from "./sources"
39	
40	// ============================================================================
41	// Types
42	// ============================================================================
43	
44	export interface RosterRow {
45	  /** 1-based rank within the source competition's division leaderboard. */
46	  sourcePlacement: number | null
47	  /** Source id that produced this row. */
48	  sourceId: string
49	  /** Source kind snapshot for UI tagging. */
50	  sourceKind: CompetitionInviteSourceKind
51	  /** Comp the athlete placed at. */
52	  sourceCompetitionId: string
53	  /** Display name of the source competition. */
54	  sourceCompetitionName: string
55	  /** Division id on the source competition. */
56	  sourceDivisionId: string
57	  /** Display label of the source division (e.g. "RX Men"). */
58	  sourceDivisionLabel: string
59	  /** Stable athlete identity. `userId` may be null if the leaderboard
60	   *  surfaced no user id (rare); `athleteName` is always present. */
61	  userId: string | null
62	  athleteName: string
63	  /** Email address for the athlete, resolved from `userTable` by `userId`.
64	   *  Null when the leaderboard surfaced no user id (rare) or the user
65	   *  has no email on file. */
66	  athleteEmail: string | null
67	  /** Invite-state columns — always null at this layer; the route attaches
68	   *  invite status via `listActiveInvitesFn` keyed by email. */
69	  inviteId: null
70	  inviteStatus: null
71	  roundId: null
72	  roundNumber: null
73	}
74	
75	export interface GetChampionshipRosterInput {
76	  championshipId: string
77	}
78	
79	// ============================================================================
80	// Internal helpers
81	// ============================================================================
82	
83	interface SourceCompetitionRef {
84	  sourceId: string
85	  sourceKind: CompetitionInviteSourceKind
86	  competitionId: string
87	  competitionName: string
88	}
89	
90	interface DivisionRef {
91	  competitionId: string
92	  competitionName: string
93	  divisionId: string
94	  divisionLabel: string
95	  sourceId: string
96	  sourceKind: CompetitionInviteSourceKind
97	}
98	
99	interface ChampionshipDivisionInfo {
100	  id: string
101	  label: string
102	}
103	
104	/**
105	 * Parse a source's `divisionMappings` JSON column into a typed array.
106	 *
107	 * The column is `text` storing
108	 * `[{ sourceDivisionId, championshipDivisionId, spots? }]`. Returns an
109	 * empty array on null / malformed JSON — the caller falls back to label
110	 * matching, which is the common case for organizers who haven't filled
111	 * in mappings yet.
112	 */
113	function parseDivisionMappings(
114	  raw: string | null | undefined,
115	): DivisionMapping[] {
116	  if (!raw) return []
117	  try {
118	    const parsed = JSON.parse(raw)
119	    if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) return []
120	    return parsed.filter(
121	      (m): m is DivisionMapping =>
122	        typeof m === "object" &&
123	        m !== null &&
124	        typeof (m as { sourceDivisionId?: unknown }).sourceDivisionId ===
125	          "string" &&
126	        typeof (m as { championshipDivisionId?: unknown })
127	          .championshipDivisionId === "string",
128	    )
129	  } catch {
130	    return []
131	  }
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Map a source-side division id to its championship-division counterpart.
136	 *
137	 * Resolution order:
138	 *   1. Explicit row in the source's `divisionMappings` JSON.
139	 *   2. Label match against the championship's divisions
140	 *      (case-insensitive, trimmed) — covers the common case where the
141	 *      source comp uses the same division labels as the championship.
142	 *
143	 * Returns `null` when no mapping can be inferred. Callers use that as
144	 * the signal to skip the cutoff (i.e. include all leaderboard rows) so
145	 * mis-mapped divisions don't silently drop qualifying athletes.
146	 */
147	function mapSourceDivisionToChampionship(args: {
148	  sourceDivisionId: string
149	  sourceDivisionLabel: string
150	  divisionMappings: DivisionMapping[]
151	  championshipDivisions: ReadonlyArray<ChampionshipDivisionInfo>
152	}): string | null {
153	  const explicit = args.divisionMappings.find(
154	    (m) => m.sourceDivisionId === args.sourceDivisionId,
155	  )
156	  if (explicit) return explicit.championshipDivisionId
157	
158	  const normalized = args.sourceDivisionLabel.trim().toLowerCase()
159	  const matched = args.championshipDivisions.find(
160	    (d) => d.label.trim().toLowerCase() === normalized,
161	  )
162	  return matched ? matched.id : null
163	}
164	
165	/**
166	 * Resolve the (competitionId, name) tuples each source contributes:
167	 * single-comp source → 1 row; series source → every comp in the group.
168	 *
169	 * Returns a deduped list keyed by `competitionId` so a comp referenced
170	 * by both a direct source AND a series source only fetches once. The
171	 * first-encountered source wins for tagging.
172	 */
173	async function resolveSourceCompetitions(
174	  championshipId: string,
175	): Promise<{
176	  sources: CompetitionInviteSource[]
177	  refs: SourceCompetitionRef[]
178	  seriesCompCountBySourceId: Map<string, number>
179	}> {
180	  const sources = await listSourcesForChampionship(championshipId)
181	  if (sources.length === 0) {
182	    return { sources: [], refs: [], seriesCompCountBySourceId: new Map() }
183	  }
184	
185	  const db = getDb()
186	
187	  // Collect all comp ids we need to look up.
188	  const directIds = sources
189	    .filter(
190	      (s): s is typeof s & { sourceCompetitionId: string } =>
191	        s.kind === "competition" && !!s.sourceCompetitionId,
192	    )
193	    .map((s) => s.sourceCompetitionId)
194	  const groupIds = sources
195	    .filter(
196	      (s): s is typeof s & { sourceGroupId: string } =>
197	        (s.kind === "series" || s.kind === "series_global") &&
198	        !!s.sourceGroupId,
199	    )
200	    .map((s) => s.sourceGroupId)
201	
202	  // One query per category; each can be empty.
203	  const [directComps, seriesComps] = await Promise.all([
204	    directIds.length > 0
205	      ? db
206	          .select({
207	            id: competitionsTable.id,
208	            name: competitionsTable.name,
209	            groupId: competitionsTable.groupId,
210	          })
211	          .from(competitionsTable)
212	          .where(inArray(competitionsTable.id, directIds))
213	      : Promise.resolve(
214	          [] as Array<{ id: string; name: string; groupId: string | null }>,
215	        ),
216	    groupIds.length > 0
217	      ? db
218	          .select({
219	            id: competitionsTable.id,
220	            name: competitionsTable.name,
221	            groupId: competitionsTable.groupId,
222	          })
223	          .from(competitionsTable)
224	          .where(inArray(competitionsTable.groupId, groupIds))
225	      : Promise.resolve(
226	          [] as Array<{ id: string; name: string; groupId: string | null }>,
227	        ),
228	  ])
229	
230	  const refs: SourceCompetitionRef[] = []
231	  const seen = new Set<string>()
232	  const seriesCompCountBySourceId = new Map<string, number>()
233	
234	  for (const source of sources) {
235	    if (source.kind === "competition" && source.sourceCompetitionId) {
236	      if (seen.has(source.sourceCompetitionId)) continue
237	      const row = directComps.find((c) => c.id === source.sourceCompetitionId)
238	      if (!row) continue
239	      seen.add(source.sourceCompetitionId)
240	      refs.push({
241	        sourceId: source.id,
242	        sourceKind: source.kind,
243	        competitionId: row.id,
244	        competitionName: row.name,
245	      })
246	    } else if (
247	      (source.kind === "series" || source.kind === "series_global") &&
248	      source.sourceGroupId
249	    ) {
250	      const matchingComps = seriesComps.filter(
251	        (c) => c.groupId === source.sourceGroupId,
252	      )
253	      seriesCompCountBySourceId.set(source.id, matchingComps.length)
254	      for (const c of matchingComps) {
255	        if (seen.has(c.id)) continue
256	        seen.add(c.id)
257	        refs.push({
258	          sourceId: source.id,
259	          sourceKind: source.kind,
260	          competitionId: c.id,
261	          competitionName: c.name,
262	        })
263	      }
264	    }
265	  }
266	  return { sources, refs, seriesCompCountBySourceId }
267	}
268	
269	/**
270	 * Resolve every (competition, division) pair to fetch a leaderboard
271	 * for. Reads `competitionsTable.settings` to find each comp's scaling
272	 * group, then resolves levels in one batched query.
273	 */
274	async function resolveDivisionRefs(
275	  comps: SourceCompetitionRef[],
276	): Promise<DivisionRef[]> {
277	  if (comps.length === 0) return []
278	
279	  const db = getDb()
280	  const compSettings = await db
281	    .select({ id: competitionsTable.id, settings: competitionsTable.settings })
282	    .from(competitionsTable)
283	    .where(
284	      inArray(
285	        competitionsTable.id,
286	        comps.map((c) => c.competitionId),
287	      ),
288	    )
289	
290	  const scalingGroupByComp = new Map<string, string>()
291	  for (const row of compSettings) {
292	    const settings = parseCompetitionSettings(row.settings)
293	    const sgid = settings?.divisions?.scalingGroupId

316	    arr.push({ id: lvl.id, label: lvl.label, position: lvl.position })

343	 * Compute the cutoff (max number of qualifying placements) for a
344	 * leaderboard fetched for `(source, sourceCompetitionId, sourceDivisionId)`.
345	 *
346	 * Single-comp source: the cutoff is the resolved per-(source,
347	 * championshipDivision) value. Override present → uses override; absent
348	 * → uses the source's `globalSpots` default (applied per division).
349	 *
350	 * Series source: keeps the full leaderboard (no cutoff). The per-
351	 * (source, division) allocation still gates invite issuing and claim
352	 * acceptance via `divisionAllocationTotals`; the leaderboard fan-out
353	 * just doesn't pre-truncate so organizers can pick whom to invite from
354	 * the full candidate pool.
355	 *
356	 * Returns `null` when no cutoff applies (i.e. include every row).
357	 */
358	function computeCutoffForLeaderboard(args: {
359	  source: CompetitionInviteSource
360	  sourceDivisionId: string
361	  sourceDivisionLabel: string
362	  championshipDivisions: ReadonlyArray<ChampionshipDivisionInfo>
363	  resolved: ResolvedSourceAllocations
364	  divisionMappings: DivisionMapping[]
365	}): number | null {
366	  if (args.source.kind === COMPETITION_INVITE_SOURCE_KIND.SERIES) {
367	    return null
368	  }
369	
370	  // Single-comp source: cutoff is the resolved spots for the mapped
371	  // championship division. If we can't infer the championship division,
372	  // fall back to no truncation so we don't accidentally drop qualifying
373	  // athletes — the downstream UI still surfaces the correct denominator.
374	  const championshipDivisionId = mapSourceDivisionToChampionship({
375	    sourceDivisionId: args.sourceDivisionId,
376	    sourceDivisionLabel: args.sourceDivisionLabel,
377	    divisionMappings: args.divisionMappings,
378	    championshipDivisions: args.championshipDivisions,
379	  })
380	  if (championshipDivisionId === null) return null
381	  const spots = args.resolved.byDivision[championshipDivisionId]
382	  if (spots === undefined) return null
383	  return spots
384	}

399	  // Fan out per (sourceComp × division) candidate fetches in parallel.
400	  // `getCandidatesForSourceComp` is purpose-built for this surface: it
401	  // returns active registrations directly, with no heat / event-status /
402	  // publication gating. The roster used to call `getCompetitionLeaderboard`
403	  // here, which silently dropped divisions whose athletes hadn't been
404	  // heat-assigned — see docs/bugs/0001-invite-candidates-missing-divisions.md.
405	  //
406	  // Cutoff is allocation budget metadata, not a candidate filter. The
407	  // organizer needs every eligible athlete on the page so they can pick
408	  // whom to invite — `entries.slice(0, cutoff)` previously dropped
409	  // eligible candidates whenever the source had more registrants than
410	  // the championship-division allocation. The denominator math lives in
411	  // `divisionAllocationTotals` outside this fan-out.
412	  const candidates = await Promise.all(
413	    divisionRefs.map((ref) =>
414	      getCandidatesForSourceComp({
415	        competitionId: ref.competitionId,
416	        divisionId: ref.divisionId,
417	      }).then((c) => ({ ref, entries: c.entries })),
418	    ),
419	  )
420	
421	  const rows: RosterRow[] = []
422	  for (const { ref, entries } of candidates) {
423	    entries.forEach((e, idx) => {
424	      rows.push({
425	        sourcePlacement: idx + 1,
426	        sourceId: ref.sourceId,
427	        sourceKind: ref.sourceKind,
428	        sourceCompetitionId: ref.competitionId,
429	        sourceCompetitionName: ref.competitionName,
430	        sourceDivisionId: ref.divisionId,
431	        sourceDivisionLabel: ref.divisionLabel,
432	        userId: e.userId,
433	        athleteName: e.athleteName,
434	        athleteEmail: e.athleteEmail,
435	        inviteId: null,
436	        inviteStatus: null,
437	        roundId: null,
438	        roundNumber: null,
439	      })
440	    })
441	  }
442	
443	  return { rows }
444	}

162	/**
163	 * Count occupied slots in a `(sourceId, championshipDivisionId)` bucket.
164	 * Mirrors the regular division-capacity pattern: a slot is occupied when
165	 * an invite has been claimed (`accepted_paid`) OR an athlete is mid-Stripe-
166	 * checkout for an invite-driven registration.
167	 *
168	 * "Mid-checkout" = a `commerce_purchases` row in `pending` with
169	 * `metadata.inviteId` pointing at an invite in this bucket and
170	 * `created_at > now - PENDING_PURCHASE_MAX_AGE_MINUTES`. The TTL matches
171	 * the Stripe checkout session expiry — abandoned sessions free their hold
172	 * implicitly without a sweep job. Pass `excludePurchaseId` to skip the
173	 * webhook's own purchase row during the authoritative race-close.
174	 *
175	 * Existing accepted-paid count + soft-hold count = the number to compare
176	 * against the bucket's allocation. Two athletes claiming the last spot
177	 * concurrently both see the hold in the count, so the second one bounces
178	 * at claim load instead of paying-then-refunding.
179	 */
180	// @lat: [[competition-invites#Claim resolution]]
181	export async function getOccupiedCountForBucket(args: {
182	  sourceId: string
183	  championshipCompetitionId: string
184	  championshipDivisionId: string
185	  excludePurchaseId?: string
186	}): Promise<number> {
187	  const db = getDb()
188	  const cutoff = new Date(
189	    Date.now() - PENDING_PURCHASE_MAX_AGE_MINUTES * 60 * 1000,
190	  )
191	
192	  const [accepted, pending] = await Promise.all([
193	    db
194	      .select({ count: sql<number>`count(*)` })
195	      .from(competitionInvitesTable)
196	      .where(
197	        and(
198	          eq(competitionInvitesTable.sourceId, args.sourceId),
199	          eq(
200	            competitionInvitesTable.championshipDivisionId,
201	            args.championshipDivisionId,
202	          ),
203	          eq(
204	            competitionInvitesTable.status,
205	            COMPETITION_INVITE_STATUS.ACCEPTED_PAID,
206	          ),
207	        ),
208	      ),
209	    db
210	      .select({ count: sql<number>`count(*)` })
211	      .from(commercePurchaseTable)
212	      .innerJoin(
213	        competitionInvitesTable,
214	        sql`${competitionInvitesTable.id} = JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(${commercePurchaseTable.metadata}, '$.inviteId'))`,
215	      )
216	      .where(
217	        and(
218	          eq(
219	            commercePurchaseTable.competitionId,
220	            args.championshipCompetitionId,
221	          ),
222	          eq(
223	            commercePurchaseTable.divisionId,
224	            args.championshipDivisionId,
225	          ),
226	          eq(commercePurchaseTable.status, COMMERCE_PURCHASE_STATUS.PENDING),
227	          gt(commercePurchaseTable.createdAt, cutoff),
228	          eq(competitionInvitesTable.sourceId, args.sourceId),
229	          args.excludePurchaseId
230	            ? sql`${commercePurchaseTable.id} != ${args.excludePurchaseId}`
231	            : sql`1 = 1`,
232	        ),
233	      ),
234	  ])
235	
236	  return Number(accepted[0]?.count ?? 0) + Number(pending[0]?.count ?? 0)
237	}

240	 * Resolve the per-(source, championship-division) allocation for one
241	 * invite. Returns `null` for bespoke invites (`sourceId IS NULL`) — the
242	 * allocation model doesn't apply to them.
243	 *
244	 * Uses {@link listAllocationsForChampionship} to find an explicit override;
245	 * falls back to the source default (computed via
246	 * {@link resolveSourceAllocations}) when no override row is present. Loads
247	 * the source row + series comp-count only when needed (the slow claim
248	 * path).
249	 */

251	export async function resolveAllocationForInvite(args: {
252	  invite: Pick<
253	    CompetitionInvite,
254	    "sourceId" | "championshipDivisionId" | "championshipCompetitionId"
255	  >
256	}): Promise<number | null> {
257	  if (!args.invite.sourceId) return null
258	
259	  const allocations = await listAllocationsForChampionship({
260	    championshipCompetitionId: args.invite.championshipCompetitionId,
261	  })
262	
263	  // Fast path: explicit override row for this (source, division).
264	  const override = allocations.find(
265	    (a) =>
266	      a.sourceId === args.invite.sourceId &&

281	    ],

283	  })
284	
285	  return resolved.byDivision[args.invite.championshipDivisionId] ?? 0

1	/**
2	 * Series Global Leaderboard Server Logic
3	 *
4	 * Computes a global leaderboard across all competitions in a series group.
5	 * Athletes from different throwdowns are ranked together by their raw performance
6	 * on the same shared workouts (matched by workoutId across comps).
7	 *
8	 * Uses series_division_mappings to translate each registration's divisionId
9	 * to a series-level division, allowing competitions with different scaling groups
10	 * to participate in the same leaderboard.
11	 */
12	
13	import { and, eq, inArray, isNotNull } from "drizzle-orm"
14	import { getDb } from "@/db"
15	import {
16	  competitionGroupsTable,
17	  competitionRegistrationsTable,
18	  competitionsTable,
19	} from "@/db/schemas/competitions"
20	import {
21	  programmingTracksTable,
22	  trackWorkoutsTable,
23	} from "@/db/schemas/programming"
24	import { scalingLevelsTable } from "@/db/schemas/scaling"
25	import { scoresTable } from "@/db/schemas/scores"
26	import { seriesDivisionMappingsTable } from "@/db/schemas/series"
27	import { userTable } from "@/db/schemas/users"
28	import { workouts } from "@/db/schemas/workouts"
29	import {
30	  calculateEventPoints,
31	  DEFAULT_SCORING_CONFIG,
32	  decodeScore,
33	  type EventScoreInput,
34	  formatScore,
35	  getDefaultScoreType,
36	  type WorkoutScheme,
37	} from "@/lib/scoring"
38	import {
39	  applyTiebreakers,
40	  type TiebreakerInput,
41	} from "@/lib/scoring/tiebreakers"
42	import { parseSeriesSettings } from "@/types/competitions"
43	import type { ScoringConfig } from "@/types/scoring"
44	
45	// ============================================================================
46	// Types
47	// ============================================================================
48	
49	export interface SeriesLeaderboardEntry {
50	  userId: string
51	  athleteName: string
52	  divisionId: string // series template scalingLevelId
53	  divisionLabel: string
54	  competitionId: string // which throwdown they competed at
55	  competitionName: string
56	  totalPoints: number
57	  overallRank: number
58	  isTeamDivision: boolean
59	  teamName: string | null
60	  eventResults: Array<{
61	    workoutId: string // the underlying shared workout ID (series event key)
62	    eventName: string
63	    scheme: string
64	    rank: number // global rank across ALL comps' athletes
65	    points: number
66	    formattedScore: string
67	    formattedTiebreak: string | null
68	  }>
69	}
70	
71	export interface SeriesLeaderboardResult {
72	  entries: SeriesLeaderboardEntry[]
73	  scoringConfig: ScoringConfig
74	  seriesEvents: Array<{ workoutId: string; name: string; scheme: string }>
75	  availableDivisions: Array<{ id: string; label: string }>
76	  unmappedCompetitions: Array<{ id: string; name: string }>
77	}
78	
79	// ============================================================================
80	// Helper
81	// ============================================================================
82	
83	function mapScoreStatus(status: string | null): EventScoreInput["status"] {
84	  switch (status) {
85	    case "scored":
86	      return "scored"
87	    case "cap":
88	      return "cap"
89	    case "dq":
90	    case "dnf":
91	      return "dnf"
92	    case "withdrawn":
93	      return "withdrawn"
94	    default:
95	      return "dns"
96	  }
97	}
98	
99	// ============================================================================
100	// Main Function
101	// ============================================================================
102	
103	export async function getSeriesLeaderboard(params: {
104	  groupId: string
105	  divisionId?: string
106	}): Promise<SeriesLeaderboardResult> {
107	  const db = getDb()
108	
109	  // 1. Load group with settings
110	  const group = await db.query.competitionGroupsTable.findFirst({
111	    where: eq(competitionGroupsTable.id, params.groupId),
112	  })
113	  if (!group) throw new Error("Series group not found")
114	
115	  const seriesSettings = parseSeriesSettings(group.settings)
116	  const scoringConfig = seriesSettings?.scoringConfig ?? DEFAULT_SCORING_CONFIG
117	  const templateGroupId = seriesSettings?.scalingGroupId
118	
119	  const emptyResult: SeriesLeaderboardResult = {
120	    entries: [],
121	    scoringConfig,
122	    seriesEvents: [],
123	    availableDivisions: [],
124	    unmappedCompetitions: [],
125	  }
126	
127	  // If no template is configured, no leaderboard is possible
128	  if (!templateGroupId) {
129	    return emptyResult
130	  }
131	
132	  // 2. Load series template divisions
133	  const templateLevels = await db
134	    .select({
135	      id: scalingLevelsTable.id,
136	      label: scalingLevelsTable.label,
137	      position: scalingLevelsTable.position,
138	      teamSize: scalingLevelsTable.teamSize,
139	    })
140	    .from(scalingLevelsTable)
141	    .where(eq(scalingLevelsTable.scalingGroupId, templateGroupId))

content.content:
[[lat.md/competition-invites#Competition Invites#Registration hand-off + Stripe workflow]] (../../lat.md/competition-invites.md:221-234)

> ## Registration hand-off + Stripe workflow
>
> [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts]]'s `initiateRegistrationPaymentFn` accepts an optional `inviteToken`. When supplied:
>
> - The token is resolved + claimability-checked + email-matched against the session — any of these failing short-circuits with an error that the registration form can surface.
> - The submitted `items` must be exactly the invited division — `items.length === 1 && items[0].divisionId === invite.championshipDivisionId`. Invites are pinned per division, so the server rejects both wrong-division submissions and "invited division plus extras". The registration form locks the UI to match (single read-only division, no multi-select) when `inviteToken` is present.
> - The registration-window check is skipped for invite-holders (invites often precede public-open or survive past close). The form mirrors this — the `!registrationOpen` disable is bypassed for both the submit button and the form inputs (division select, affiliate, team fields, waiver toggles, question fields) when the invite lock is active. Otherwise the submit unlocks but the required fields stay frozen, and the invitee can't actually fill anything in.
> - The single item's purchase metadata gets `inviteId` tagged so the Stripe workflow can flip the invite on payment success.
> - The per-(source, division) allocation guardrail runs before the Stripe session is created — same `assertInviteWithinAllocation` + `getOccupiedCountForBucket` pair the claim landing uses. This closes the `/register?invite=…` direct-submit hole where an athlete with a valid invite for an already-full bucket would otherwise pay-and-refund. Bespoke invites (sourceId IS NULL) bypass.
>
> [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/workflows/stripe-checkout-workflow.ts]] reads `inviteId` from the purchase metadata inside `create-registration`, threads it through `RegistrationStepResult`, and runs a new `update-competition-invite-status` step after registration creation. The step flips the invite to `accepted_paid`, sets `paidAt`, sets `claimedRegistrationId`, and nulls `claimToken` so a replay of the original email link short-circuits. Idempotent via the `status = "pending"` guard on the update predicate so a workflow retry doesn't double-flip. `processCheckoutInline` (local-dev path) runs the same helper to keep behavior consistent without Cloudflare Workflows.
>
> When a 100%-off coupon zeroes the total, the registration server fn takes the `allFree` branch and skips Stripe entirely — so it mirrors the workflow flip inline right after creating the registration row. Without this, an invite-locked free checkout would leave the invite stuck in `pending` even though the athlete is registered.
>

> - The submitted `items` must be exactly the invited division — `items.length === 1 && items[0].divisionId === invite.championshipDivisionId`. Invites are pinned per division, so the server rejects both wrong-division submissions and "invited division plus extras". The registration form locks the UI to match (single read-only division, no multi-select) when `inviteToken` is present.
> - The registration-window check is skipped for invite-holders (invites often precede public-open or survive past close). The form mirrors this — the `!registrationOpen` disable is bypassed for both the submit button and the form inputs (division select, affiliate, team fields, waiver toggles, question fields) when the invite lock is active. > - The per-(source, division) allocation guardrail runs before the Stripe session is created — same `assertInviteWithinAllocation` + `getOccupiedCountForBucket` pair the claim landing uses. >

The step flips the invite to `accepted_paid`, sets `paidAt`, sets `claimedRegistrationId`, and nulls `claimToken` so a replay of the original email link short-circuits. Idempotent via the `status = "pending"` guard on the update predicate so a workflow retry doesn't double-flip. `processCheckoutInline` (local-dev path) runs the same helper to keep behavior consistent without Cloudflare Workflows.
>
> When a 100%-off coupon zeroes the total, the registration server fn takes the `allFree` branch and skips Stripe entirely — so it mirrors the workflow flip inline right after creating the registration row. Without this, an invite-locked free checkout would leave the invite stuck in `pending` even though the athlete is registered.

## Registration hand-off + Stripe workflow

[[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts]]'s `initiateRegistrationPaymentFn` accepts an optional `inviteToken`. When supplied:

- The token is resolved + claimability-checked + email-matched against the session — any of these failing short-circuits with an error that the registration form can surface.
- The submitted `items` must be exactly the invited division — `items.length === 1 && items[0].divisionId === invite.championshipDivisionId`. Invites are pinned per division, so the server rejects both wrong-division submissions and "invited division plus extras". The registration form locks the UI to match (single read-only division, no multi-select) when `inviteToken` is present.
- The registration-window check is skipped for invite-holders (invites often precede public-open or survive past close). The form mirrors this — the `!registrationOpen` disable is bypassed for both the submit button and the form inputs (division select, affiliate, team fields, waiver toggles, question fields) when the invite lock is active. Otherwise the submit unlocks but the required fields stay frozen, and the invitee can't actually fill anything in.
- The single item's purchase metadata gets `inviteId` tagged so the Stripe workflow can flip the invite on payment success.
- The per-(source, division) allocation guardrail runs before the Stripe session is created — same `assertInviteWithinAllocation` + `getOccupiedCountForBucket` pair the claim landing uses. This closes the `/register?invite=…` direct-submit hole where an athlete with a valid invite for an already-full bucket would otherwise pay-and-refund. Bespoke invites (sourceId IS NULL) bypass.

[[apps/wodsmith-start/src/workflows/stripe-checkout-workflow.ts]] reads `inviteId` from the purchase metadata inside `create-registration`, threads it through `RegistrationStepResult`, and runs a new `update-competition-invite-status` step after registration creation. The step flips the invite to `accepted_paid`, sets `paidAt`, sets `claimedRegistrationId`, and nulls `claimToken` so a replay of the original email link short-circuits. Idempotent via the `status = "pending"` guard on the update predicate so a workflow retry doesn't double-flip. `processCheckoutInline` (local-dev path) runs the same helper to keep behavior consistent without Cloudflare Workflows.

When a 100%-off coupon zeroes the total, the registration server fn takes the `allFree` branch and skips Stripe entirely — so it mirrors the workflow flip inline right after creating the registration row. Without this, an invite-locked free checkout would leave the invite stuck in `pending` even though the athlete is registered.

input.new_string:
When a 100%-off coupon zeroes the total, the registration server fn takes the `allFree` branch and skips Stripe entirely — so it mirrors the workflow flip inline right after creating the registration row. Without this, an invite-locked free checkout would leave the invite stuck in `pending` even though the athlete is registered.

The free-path flip runs the same `resolveAllocationForInvite` + `getOccupiedCountForBucket` + `assertInviteWithinAllocation` triple a second time, immediately before the inline `accepted_paid` update. The preflight alone is insufficient here: the free path skips Stripe, so neither concurrent free claim shows up in `getOccupiedCountForBucket`'s in-flight purchase count, and both can pass the preflight. The write-time recheck narrows the race window to whoever flips first; the loser rolls back its just-created registrations to `REMOVED` (tracked via `createdRegistrationIds`) so they don't squat division capacity, and surfaces the same "all spots filled" error. This isn't atomic — true atomicity would need a DB-level slot constraint — but it bounds the window to roughly one read round-trip.
